NKJV.1: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, To Philemon our beloved friend and fellow laborer,
NKJV.2: to the beloved Apphia, Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church in your house:
NKJV.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.4: I thank my God, making mention of you always in my prayers,
NKJV.5: hearing of your love and faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints,
NKJV.6: that the sharing of your faith may become effective by the acknowledgment of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.7: For we have great joy and consolation in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed by you, brother.
NKJV.8: Therefore, though I might be very bold in Christ to command you what is fitting,
NKJV.9: yet for love’s sake I rather appeal to you—being such a one as Paul, the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ—
NKJV.10: I appeal to you for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten while in my chains,
NKJV.11: who once was unprofitable to you, but now is profitable to you and to me.
NKJV.12: I am sending him back. You therefore receive him, that is, my own heart,
NKJV.13: whom I wished to keep with me, that on your behalf he might minister to me in my chains for the gospel.
NKJV.14: But without your consent I wanted to do nothing, that your good deed might not be by compulsion, as it were, but voluntary.
NKJV.15: For perhaps he departed for a while for this purpose, that you might receive him forever,
NKJV.16: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—a beloved brother, especially to me but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
NKJV.17: If then you count me as a partner, receive him as you would me.
NKJV.18: But if he has wronged you or owes anything, put that on my account.
NKJV.19: I, Paul, am writing with my own hand. I will repay—not to mention to you that you owe me even your own self besides.
NKJV.20: Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord; refresh my heart in the Lord.
NKJV.21: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
NKJV.22: But, meanwhile, also prepare a guest room for me, for I trust that through your prayers I shall be granted to you.
NKJV.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
NKJV.24: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, Luke, my fellow laborers.
NKJV.25: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

Amen<S>281</S>. <sup>Written<S>1125</S> from<S>575</S> Rome<S>4516</S> to<S>4314</S> Philemon<S>5371</S>, by<S>1223</S> Onesimus<S>3682</S> a servant<S>3610</S>.</sup>

ESV.1: Paul, a prisoner for Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, To Philemon our beloved fellow worker
ESV.2: and Apphia our sister and Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church in your house:
ESV.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.4: I thank my God always when I remember you in my prayers,
ESV.5: because I hear of your love and of the faith that you have toward the Lord Jesus and for all the saints,
ESV.6: and I pray that the sharing of your faith may become effective for the full knowledge of every good thing that is in us for the sake of Christ.
ESV.7: For I have derived much joy and comfort from your love, my brother, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you.
ESV.8: Accordingly, though I am bold enough in Christ to command you to do what is required,
ESV.9: yet for love's sake I prefer to appeal to you — I, Paul, an old man and now a prisoner also for Christ Jesus —
ESV.10: I appeal to you for my child, Onesimus,  whose father I became in my imprisonment.
ESV.11: (Formerly he was useless to you, but now he is indeed useful to you and to me.)
ESV.12: I am sending him back to you, sending my very heart.
ESV.13: I would have been glad to keep him with me, in order that he might serve me on your behalf during my imprisonment for the gospel,
ESV.14: but I preferred to do nothing without your consent in order that your goodness might not be by compulsion but of your own accord.
ESV.15: For this perhaps is why he was parted from you for a while, that you might have him back forever,
ESV.16: no longer as a bondservant  but more than a bondservant, as a beloved brother — especially to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
ESV.17: So if you consider me your partner, receive him as you would receive me.
ESV.18: If he has wronged you at all, or owes you anything, charge that to my account.
ESV.19: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it — to say nothing of your owing me even your own self.
ESV.20: Yes, brother, I want some benefit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
ESV.21: Confident of your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
ESV.22: At the same time, prepare a guest room for me, for I am hoping that through your prayers I will be graciously given to you.
ESV.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends greetings to you,
ESV.24: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
ESV.25: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

NASB.1: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, To Philemon our beloved [brother] and fellow worker,
NASB.2: and to Apphia our sister, and to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church in your house:
NASB.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.4: I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,
NASB.5: because I hear of your love and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints;
NASB.6: [and I pray] that the fellowship of your faith may become effective through the knowledge of every good thing which is in you for Christ's sake.
NASB.7: For I have come to have much joy and comfort in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.
NASB.8: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to order you [to do] what is proper,
NASB.9: yet for love's sake I rather appeal [to you]-- since I am such a person as Paul, the aged, and now also a prisoner of Christ Jesus--
NASB.10: I appeal to you for my child Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my imprisonment,
NASB.11: who formerly was useless to you, but now is useful both to you and to me.
NASB.12: I have sent him back to you in person, that is, [sending] my very heart,
NASB.13: whom I wished to keep with me, so that on your behalf he might minister to me in my imprisonment for the gospel;
NASB.14: but without your consent I did not want to do anything, so that your goodness would not be, in effect, by compulsion but of your own free will.
NASB.15: For perhaps he was for this reason separated [from you] for a while, that you would have him back forever,
NASB.16: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
NASB.17: If then you regard me a partner, accept him as [you would] me.
NASB.18: But if he has wronged you in any way or owes you anything, charge that to my account;
NASB.19: I, Paul, am writing this with my own hand, I will repay it (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self as well).
NASB.20: Yes, brother, let me benefit from you in the Lord; refresh my heart in Christ.
NASB.21: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, since I know that you will do even more than what I say.
NASB.22: At the same time also prepare me a lodging, for I hope that through your prayers I will be given to you.
NASB.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
NASB.24: [as do] Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, Luke, my fellow workers.
NASB.25: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, a prisoner for preaching the Good News about Christ Jesus, and from our brother Timothy. I am writing to Philemon, our beloved co-worker,
NLT.2: and to our sister Apphia, and to our fellow soldier Archippus, and to the church that meets in your house.
NLT.3: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.4: I always thank my God when I pray for you, Philemon,
NLT.5: because I keep hearing about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all of God’s people.
NLT.6: And I am praying that you will put into action the generosity that comes from your faith as you understand and experience all the good things we have in Christ.
NLT.7: Your love has given me much joy and comfort, my brother, for your kindness has often refreshed the hearts of God’s people.
NLT.8: That is why I am boldly asking a favor of you. I could demand it in the name of Christ because it is the right thing for you to do.
NLT.9: But because of our love, I prefer simply to ask you. Consider this as a request from me — Paul, an old man and now also a prisoner for the sake of Christ Jesus.
NLT.10: I appeal to you to show kindness to my child, Onesimus. I became his father in the faith while here in prison.
NLT.11: Onesimus hasn’t been of much use to you in the past, but now he is very useful to both of us.
NLT.12: I am sending him back to you, and with him comes my own heart.
NLT.13: I wanted to keep him here with me while I am in these chains for preaching the Good News, and he would have helped me on your behalf.
NLT.14: But I didn’t want to do anything without your consent. I wanted you to help because you were willing, not because you were forced.
NLT.15: It seems you lost Onesimus for a little while so that you could have him back forever.
NLT.16: He is no longer like a slave to you. He is more than a slave, for he is a beloved brother, especially to me. Now he will mean much more to you, both as a man and as a brother in the Lord.
NLT.17: So if you consider me your partner, welcome him as you would welcome me.
NLT.18: If he has wronged you in any way or owes you anything, charge it to me.
NLT.19: I, PAUL, WRITE THIS WITH MY OWN HAND: I WILL REPAY IT. AND I WON’T MENTION THAT YOU OWE ME YOUR VERY SOUL!
NLT.20: Yes, my brother, please do me this favor for the Lord’s sake. Give me this encouragement in Christ.
NLT.21: I am confident as I write this letter that you will do what I ask and even more!
NLT.22: One more thing — please prepare a guest room for me, for I am hoping that God will answer your prayers and let me return to you soon.
NLT.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends you his greetings.
NLT.24: So do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my co-workers.
NLT.25: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

WEB.1: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved fellow worker,
WEB.2: to the beloved Apphia, to Archippus, our fellow soldier, and to the assembly in your house:
WEB.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.4: I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,
WEB.5: hearing of your love, and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all the saints;
WEB.6: that the fellowship of your faith may become effective, in the knowledge of every good thing which is in us in Christ Jesus.
WEB.7: For we have much joy and comfort in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.
WEB.8: Therefore though I have all boldness in Christ to command you that which is appropriate,
WEB.9: yet for love’s sake I rather beg, being such a one as Paul, the aged, but also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.
WEB.10: I beg you for my child, whom I have become the father of in my chains, Onesimus,
WEB.11: who once was useless to you, but now is useful to you and to me.
WEB.12: I am sending him back. Therefore receive him, that is, my own heart,
WEB.13: whom I desired to keep with me, that on your behalf he might serve me in my chains for the Good News.
WEB.14: But I was willing to do nothing without your consent, that your goodness would not be as of necessity, but of free will.
WEB.15: For perhaps he was therefore separated from you for a while, that you would have him forever,
WEB.16: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much rather to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
WEB.17: If then you count me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.
WEB.18: But if he has wronged you at all, or owes you anything, put that to my account.
WEB.19: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self besides).
WEB.20: Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in the Lord.
WEB.21: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even beyond what I say.
WEB.22: Also, prepare a guest room for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.
WEB.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
WEB.24: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
WEB.25: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

CSB17.1: Paul, <sup>ⓚ</sup> a prisoner <sup>ⓛ</sup> of Christ Jesus, <sup>ⓜ</sup> and Timothy <sup>ⓝ</sup> our brother: To Philemon our dear friend and coworker,
CSB17.2: to Apphia our sister, <sup>[1]</sup> to Archippus <sup>ⓐ</sup> our fellow soldier, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and to the church that meets in your home. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.4: I always thank my God when I mention you in my prayers,
CSB17.5: because I hear of your love <sup>ⓓ</sup> for all the saints and the faith that you have in the Lord Jesus.
CSB17.6: I pray that your participation in the faith may become effective <sup>ⓔ</sup> through knowing every good thing <sup>ⓕ</sup> that is in us <sup>[2]</sup> for the glory of Christ.
CSB17.7: For I have great joy and encouragement from your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed <sup>ⓖ</sup> through you, brother.
CSB17.8: For this reason, although I have great boldness in Christ to command you to do what is right,
CSB17.9: I appeal to you, instead, on the basis of love. I, Paul, as an elderly man <sup>[3]</sup> and now also as a prisoner <sup>ⓗ</sup> of Christ Jesus,
CSB17.10: appeal to you for my son, <sup>ⓘ</sup> Onesimus. <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> I became his father <sup>ⓚ</sup> while I was in chains.
CSB17.11: Once he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
CSB17.12: I am sending him back to you — I am sending my very own heart. <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.13: I wanted to keep him with me, so that in my imprisonment for the gospel he might serve me in your place.
CSB17.14: But I didn’t want to do anything without your consent, so that your good deed might not be out of obligation, but of your own free will.
CSB17.15: For perhaps this is why he was separated from you for a brief time, so that you might get him back permanently,
CSB17.16: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave — as a dearly loved brother. He is especially so to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh <sup>ⓛ</sup> and in the Lord. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.17: So if you consider me a partner, welcome <sup>ⓝ</sup> him as you would me.
CSB17.18: And if he has wronged you in any way, or owes you anything, charge that to my account. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.19: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: <sup>ⓑ</sup> I will repay it — not to mention to you that you owe me even your very self.
CSB17.20: Yes, brother, may I benefit from you in the Lord; refresh my heart in Christ.
CSB17.21: Since I am confident of your obedience, I am writing to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
CSB17.22: Meanwhile, also prepare a guest room for me, since I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.
CSB17.23: Epaphras, <sup>ⓒ</sup> my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends you greetings, and so do
CSB17.24: Mark, <sup>ⓓ</sup> Aristarchus, <sup>ⓔ</sup> Demas, and Luke, <sup>ⓕ</sup> my coworkers.
CSB17.25: The grace of the Lord <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup> Jesus Christ be with your spirit. <sup>ⓗ</sup>

AMP.1:
Paul, a prisoner [for the sake] of Christ Jesus (the Messiah, the Anointed), and our brother Timothy,
To Philemon our dearly beloved friend and fellow worker,
AMP.2: and to [your wife] Apphia our sister, and to Archippus our fellow soldier [in ministry], and to the church that meets in your house:
AMP.3: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.4: I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,
AMP.5: because I hear of your love and of your faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints (God's people).
AMP.6: I pray that the sharing of your faith may become effective and powerful because of your accurate knowledge of every good thing which is ours in Christ.
AMP.7: For I have had great joy and comfort and encouragement from your love, because the hearts of the saints (God's people) have been refreshed through you, my brother.
AMP.8: Therefore [on the basis of these facts], though I have enough confidence in Christ to order you to do what is appropriate,
AMP.9: yet for love's sake I prefer to appeal to you—since I am such a person as Paul, an old man, and now also a prisoner [for the sake] of Christ Jesus—
AMP.10: I appeal to you for my [own spiritual] child Onesimus, whom I have fathered [in the faith] while a captive in these chains.
AMP.11: Once he was useless to you, but now he is indeed useful to you as well as to me.
AMP.12: I have sent him back to you in person, that is, like sending my very heart.
AMP.13: I would have chosen to keep him with me, so that he might minister to me on your behalf during my imprisonment for the gospel;
AMP.14: but I did not want to do anything without first getting your consent, so that your goodness would not be, in effect, by compulsion but of your own free will.
AMP.15: Perhaps it was for this reason that he was separated from you for a while, so that you would have him back forever,
AMP.16: no longer as a slave, but [as someone] more than a slave, as a brother [in Christ], especially dear to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh [as a servant] and in the Lord [as a fellow believer].
AMP.17: So if you consider me a partner, welcome and accept him as you would me.
AMP.18: But if he has wronged you in any way or owes you anything, charge that to my account;
AMP.19: I, Paul, write this with my own hand, I will repay it in full (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self as well).
AMP.20: Yes, brother, let me have some benefit and joy from you in the Lord; refresh my heart in Christ.
AMP.21: I write to you [perfectly] confident of your obedient compliance, since I know that you will do even more than I ask.
AMP.22: At the same time also prepare a guest room for me [in expectation of a visit], for I hope that through your prayers I will be [granted the gracious privilege of] coming to you [at Colossae].
AMP.23: Greetings to you from Epaphras, my fellow prisoner here in [the cause of] Christ Jesus,
AMP.24: and from Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
AMP.25: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

MSG.1: I, Paul, am a prisoner for the sake of Christ, here with my brother Timothy. I write this letter to you, Philemon, my good friend and companion in this work—
MSG.2: also to our sister Apphia, to Archippus, a real trooper, and to the church that meets in your house.
MSG.3: God's best to you! Christ's blessings on you!
MSG.4: Every time your name comes up in my prayers, I say, "Oh, thank you, God!"
MSG.5: I keep hearing of the love and faith you have for the Master Jesus, which brims over to other Christians.
MSG.6: And I keep praying that this faith we hold in common keeps showing up in the good things we do, and that people recognize Christ in all of it.
MSG.7: Friend, you have no idea how good your love makes me feel, doubly so when I see your hospitality to fellow believers.
MSG.8: In line with all this I have a favor to ask of you. As Christ's ambassador and now a prisoner for him, I wouldn't hesitate to command this if I thought it necessary,
MSG.9: but I'd rather make it a personal request.
MSG.10: While here in jail, I've fathered a child, so to speak. And here he is, hand-carrying this letter—Onesimus!
MSG.11: He was useless to you before; now he's useful to both of us.
MSG.12: I'm sending him back to you, but it feels like I'm cutting off my right arm in doing so.
MSG.13: I wanted in the worst way to keep him here as your stand-in to help out while I'm in jail for the Message.
MSG.14: But I didn't want to do anything behind your back, make you do a good deed that you hadn't willingly agreed to.
MSG.15: Maybe it's all for the best that you lost him for a while. You're getting him back now for good—
MSG.16: and no mere slave this time, but a true Christian brother! That's what he was to me—he'll be even more than that to you.
MSG.17: So if you still consider me a comrade-in-arms, welcome him back as you would me.
MSG.18: If he damaged anything or owes you anything, chalk it up to my account.
MSG.19: This is my personal signature—Paul—and I stand behind it. (I don't need to remind you, do I, that you owe your very life to me?)
MSG.20: Do me this big favor, friend. You'll be doing it for Christ, but it will also do my heart good.
MSG.21: I know you well enough to know you will. You'll probably go far beyond what I've written.
MSG.22: And by the way, get a room ready for me. Because of your prayers, I fully expect to be your guest again.
MSG.23: Epaphras, my cellmate in the cause of Christ, says hello.
MSG.24: Also my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
MSG.25: All the best to you from the Master, Jesus Christ!

NIV.1:
Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother,
To Philemon our dear friend and fellow worker,
NIV.2: to Apphia our sister, to Archippus our fellow soldier and to the church that meets in your home:
NIV.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.4:
Thanksgiving and Prayer
I always thank my God as I remember you in my prayers,
NIV.5: because I hear about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints.
NIV.6: I pray that you may be active in sharing your faith, so that you will have a full understanding of every good thing we have in Christ.
NIV.7: Your love has given me great joy and encouragement, because you, brother, have refreshed the hearts of the saints.
NIV.8:
Paul’s Plea for Onesimus
Therefore, although in Christ I could be bold and order you to do what you ought to do,
NIV.9: yet I appeal to you on the basis of love. I then, as Paul—an old man and now also a prisoner of Christ Jesus—
NIV.10: I appeal to you for my son Onesimus, who became my son while I was in chains.
NIV.11: Formerly he was useless to you, but now he has become useful both to you and to me.
NIV.12: I am sending him—who is my very heart—back to you.
NIV.13: I would have liked to keep him with me so that he could take your place in helping me while I am in chains for the gospel.
NIV.14: But I did not want to do anything without your consent, so that any favor you do will be spontaneous and not forced.
NIV.15: Perhaps the reason he was separated from you for a little while was that you might have him back for good—
NIV.16: no longer as a slave, but better than a slave, as a dear brother. He is very dear to me but even dearer to you, both as a man and as a brother in the Lord.
NIV.17: So if you consider me a partner, welcome him as you would welcome me.
NIV.18: If he has done you any wrong or owes you anything, charge it to me.
NIV.19: I, Paul, am writing this with my own hand. I will pay it back—not to mention that you owe me your very self.
NIV.20: I do wish, brother, that I may have some benefit from you in the Lord; refresh my heart in Christ.
NIV.21: Confident of your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I ask.
NIV.22: And one thing more: Prepare a guest room for me, because I hope to be restored to you in answer to your prayers.
NIV.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends you greetings.
NIV.24: And so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas and Luke, my fellow workers.
NIV.25: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, To Philemon our dear friend and co-worker,
NRSVCE.2: to Apphia our sister, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church in your house:
NRSVCE.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.4: When I remember you in my prayers, I always thank my God
NRSVCE.5: because I hear of your love for all the saints and your faith toward the Lord Jesus.
NRSVCE.6: I pray that the sharing of your faith may become effective when you perceive all the good that we may do for Christ.
NRSVCE.7: I have indeed received much joy and encouragement from your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, my brother.
NRSVCE.8: For this reason, though I am bold enough in Christ to command you to do your duty,
NRSVCE.9: yet I would rather appeal to you on the basis of love — and I, Paul, do this as an old man, and now also as a prisoner of Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.10: I am appealing to you for my child, Onesimus, whose father I have become during my imprisonment.
NRSVCE.11: Formerly he was useless to you, but now he is indeed useful both to you and to me.
NRSVCE.12: I am sending him, that is, my own heart, back to you.
NRSVCE.13: I wanted to keep him with me, so that he might be of service to me in your place during my imprisonment for the gospel;
NRSVCE.14: but I preferred to do nothing without your consent, in order that your good deed might be voluntary and not something forced.
NRSVCE.15: Perhaps this is the reason he was separated from you for a while, so that you might have him back forever,
NRSVCE.16: no longer as a slave but more than a slave, a beloved brother — especially to me but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
NRSVCE.17: So if you consider me your partner, welcome him as you would welcome me.
NRSVCE.18: If he has wronged you in any way, or owes you anything, charge that to my account.
NRSVCE.19: I, Paul, am writing this with my own hand: I will repay it. I say nothing about your owing me even your own self.
NRSVCE.20: Yes, brother, let me have this benefit from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
NRSVCE.21: Confident of your obedience, I am writing to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
NRSVCE.22: One thing more — prepare a guest room for me, for I am hoping through your prayers to be restored to you.
NRSVCE.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends greetings to you,
NRSVCE.24: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
NRSVCE.25: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

NET.1: From Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our dear friend and colaborer,
NET.2: to Apphia our sister, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church that meets in your house.
NET.3: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.4: I always thank my God as I remember you in my prayers,
NET.5: because I hear of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints.
NET.6: I pray that the faith you share with us may deepen your understanding of every blessing that belongs to you in Christ.
NET.7: I have had great joy and encouragement because of your love, for the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.
NET.8: So, although I have quite a lot of confidence in Christ and could command you to do what is proper,
NET.9: I would rather appeal to you on the basis of love — I, Paul, an old man and even now a prisoner for the sake of Christ Jesus —
NET.10: I am appealing to you concerning my child, whose spiritual father I have become during my imprisonment, that is, Onesimus,
NET.11: who was formerly useless to you, but is now useful to you and me.
NET.12: I have sent him (who is my very heart) back to you.
NET.13: I wanted to keep him so that he could serve me in your place during my imprisonment for the sake of the gospel.
NET.14: However, without your consent I did not want to do anything, so that your good deed would not be out of compulsion, but from your own willingness.
NET.15: For perhaps it was for this reason that he was separated from you for a little while, so that you would have him back eternally,
NET.16: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, as a dear brother. He is especially so to me, and even more so to you now, both humanly speaking and in the Lord.
NET.17: Therefore if you regard me as a partner, accept him as you would me.
NET.18: Now if he has defrauded you of anything or owes you anything, charge what he owes to me.
NET.19: I, Paul, have written this letter with my own hand: I will repay it. I could also mention that you owe me your very self.
NET.20: Yes, brother, let me have some benefit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
NET.21: Since I was confident that you would obey, I wrote to you, because I knew that you would do even more than what I am asking you to do.
NET.22: At the same time also, prepare a place for me to stay, for I hope that through your prayers I will be given back to you.
NET.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you.
NET.24: Mark, Aristarchus, Demas and Luke, my colaborers, greet you too.
NET.25: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
